scope: Artifact-only remediation plan compiler review packet for IF09-FIND-001 S3.
remediation_objectives: Ensure stale project SHA metadata cannot enter canonical active-context state without explicit drift recording or fail-closed handling.
Bind canonical packet generation to verified primary refs before any future state write is considered valid.
Preserve the forensic evidence chain for SHA mismatches instead of silently normalizing or discarding mismatch evidence.
Prepare a future remediation scope that can be proven locally with regression evidence before any Infernus revalidation request.
proposed_remediation_strategy: Harden the canonical sync packet assembly path so the latest completed project SHA is derived from verified primary refs before a packet is written or promoted.
Introduce an explicit fail-closed branch when recorded SHA and verified SHA diverge, unless a dedicated drift artifact is emitted and linked into the evidence chain.
Treat mismatch preservation as a first-class output of the remediation flow so the system records drift explicitly rather than silently overwriting packet lineage.
Anchor future proof of remediation on the IF10 regression plan for tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py and on validator-confirmed no-real-surface locks.
candidate_change_surface: Canonical sync packet generation logic responsible for latest_completed_project_commit_sha selection and persistence.
Primary-ref verification path that compares the recorded project SHA against the verified canonical ref before active-context packet acceptance.
Drift artifact emission or fail-closed decision path for SHA mismatch handling.
Regression coverage defined by IF10-REG-001 at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py.
Supporting active-context governance documentation and evidence artifacts that explain mismatch handling without opening runtime or product scope.
non_goals: Apply any patch or code change in this gate.
Execute remediation, runtime, product, Bedrock, real_apply, or secret-bearing flows.
Open Infernus revalidation in this gate.
Rename roadmap phases or alter ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md.
acceptance_criteria_for_future_remediati: The future remediation must prove that latest_completed_project_commit_sha is derived from the verified primary ref chosen for the canonical packet, not from stale packet metadata.
A SHA mismatch must either emit an explicit drift artifact linked to the evidence chain or fail closed; silent SHA normalization is not acceptable.

The future regression test at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py must pass with assertions covering canonical SHA equality, mismatch fail-closed or drift-emission behavior, and preservation of forbidden execution surfaces as false.
Validator output must remain pass after the remediation artifacts are materialized, with no structural schema drift forced into ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json.

The remediation proof must keep real execution, remediation apply, patch apply, finding close, runtime real, real apply, secrets, product, and Bedrock authorization false unless a later explicit gate changes them.
The future remediation packet must include before/after evidence that the canonical packet no longer retains stale project SHA metadata while still preserving custody of any mismatch evidence.
required_evidence_for_future_remediation: A remediation decision artifact that names the exact canonical sync control being changed and why it satisfies IF10-REM-001.
Regression evidence for IF10-REG-001, including the test file path tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py and pass results for the required assertions.
A before/after packet comparison showing the stale-SHA condition from IF09-FIND-001 and the corrected canonical SHA behavior after remediation.
A drift artifact or fail-closed evidence sample demonstrating that mismatches are surfaced explicitly rather than normalized away.
Validator pass evidence and a no-real-execution / no-real-apply attestation for the remediation proof gate.
An updated evidence chain linking the remediation proof back to IF09-FIND-001, IF10-RCA-001, IF10-REM-001, and IF10-REG-001.
rollback_or_abort_criteria: Abort if the future remediation cannot identify a single verified primary ref selection rule for the canonical packet.
Abort if the proposed change requires mutating runtime, product, Bedrock, secret, network, or real_apply locks.
Abort if the remediation cannot preserve mismatch evidence explicitly and instead depends on silent normalization.
Abort if regression coverage for IF10-REG-001 is missing, ambiguous, or fails.
Abort if validator pass depends on structural schema drift in ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json or ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json outside the approved scope.
residual_risks: This packet is planning-only; it does not prove the remediation is implemented or effective yet.
The live ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json still carries the stale next-step token because schema and validator coupling make a safe state mutation out of scope here.
Future remediation may span both packet-generation logic and governance validation surfaces, increasing coordination risk even if runtime remains closed.
IF09-FIND-001 remains open until a later remediation proof and an Infernus revalidation wave both succeed.
future_revalidation_requirements: A future Infernus revalidation request must target IF10-REVAL-001 only after remediation proof is complete.
The revalidation success criteria must confirm that the corrected packet emits canonical project SHA without primary-ref drift.
The revalidation success criteria must confirm validator pass with no SHA-mismatch warning for the corrected packet.
The revalidation evidence must confirm that runtime, network, secret, MCP, RAG, memory, socket, shell, filesystem, cost, quota, and audio locks remain false.
